It's not on the root, maybe it's somewhere else, but I'm just generally surprised because it's not being applied. You can see, for instance, the Hoff OS, so H-O-F-O-S repository. There we use it and you can see that Please just copy the conservative CSS design system from the Hoff OS and just look under my repos.

Directory to see which kind of directories we have and apply it so that when I restart, make dev, we use the conservative design system. Obviously, I would want to be able to switch this. So it should be some ENV variable or something different. I don't know. But yeah, maybe I start to make dev with the design system argument or something, but I just want to see that we could apply something like this.  (~/repos/hof-os for instance)

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

.PHONY: help install dev dev-api dev-web kill-ports seed \
	db-up db-down db-reset db-logs \
	test test-py test-js format format-check python-format-check lint architecture \
	typecheck verify build clean

help:
--
	@echo "  dev       — db-up + API + Web (:$(API_PORT) / :$(WEB_PORT))"
	@echo "  verify    — format-check, lint, architecture, tc, pytest, vite build"

install:
	$(PNPM) install
	cd $(APP_DIR) && $(PYTHON) -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip && .venv/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"

db-up:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up -d
	@echo "Postgres localhost:5545  Redis localhost:6392  MinIO http://localhost:9120"

--
	    pids=$$(lsof -ti :$$p 2>/dev/null); \
	    [ -n "$$pids" ] && kill -9 $$pids 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  done; \
	  pkill -9 -f "vite.*$$WS_TAG"     2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "uvicorn.*$$WS_TAG"   2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "concurrently.*$$WS_TAG" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "turbo run dev"       2>/dev/null || true; \
	  busy=""; \
	  for p in $$PORTS; do \
	    lsof -ti :$$p >/dev/null 2>&1 && busy="$$busy $$p"; \
	  done; \
	  [ -z "$$busy" ] && exit 0; \
	  sleep 0.5; \
	done; \
	echo "kill-ports: still in use after retries:$$busy" >&2; \
--
dev: kill-ports seed
else
dev: db-up kill-ports seed
endif
	@echo "→ API   http://127.0.0.1:$(API_PORT)/api/health"
	@echo "→ Web   http://127.0.0.1:$(WEB_PORT)"
	$(PNPM) -w exec concurrently -k -n api,web -c blue,magenta \
	  "$(MAKE) dev-api" \
	  "$(MAKE) dev-web"

dev-api:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn bim_ai.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port $(API_PORT) --reload

dev-web:
	API_PORT=$(API_PORT) $(PNPM) --filter @bim-ai/web dev --port $(WEB_PORT) --host 127.0.0.1 --strictPort

seed:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python scripts/seed.py

test: test-py test-js

aiTitle: Apply conservative CSS design system to project

content.text:
Done. Here's the full picture:

**What was already there** — the wiring was complete: `index.css` → `@bim-ai-design-system.css` (Vite alias) → `conservative.css` or `default.css` depending on `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM`. The design tokens package already had the full slate/navy palette with Source Serif 4.

**What was missing** — the Makefile's `dev-web` target never forwarded the env var, so `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM` was always unset (defaulting to `default`). And the font wasn't being loaded.

**What I changed:**
1. `Makefile` — added `DESIGN_SYSTEM ?= default` variable and forwards it as `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=$(DESIGN_SYSTEM)` in `dev-web`
2. `packages/web/src/design-systems/conservative.css` — added Google Fonts import for Source Serif 4

**To use it:**
```bash
DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative make dev
```

Or for just the web process:
```bash
DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative make dev-web
```

## Summary of Canvas Overlay Components

I've thoroughly searched the `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src` directory and located all the canvas overlay components. Here are the exact file paths and line numbers:

### 1. **ViewCube Component** (3D orientation cube showing FRONT/BACK/LEFT/RIGHT/TOP/BOTTOM)

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/ViewCube.tsx`
- **Main component**: Line 62-156
- **Face labels definition**: Line 53-60 (defines FRONT, BACK, LEFT, RIGHT, TOP, BOTTOM faces)
- **FRONT label**: Line 54 specifically
- **Compass subcomponent**: Line 190-204 (displays cardinal direction N/E/S/W)

**Helper file**:
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/viewCubeAlignment.ts` (pure math module for ViewCube alignment logic)
  - ViewCubeFace type definition: Line 11
  - Face azimuth constants: Line 49-56
  - `alignmentForPick()` function: Line 86-97

### 2. **"Walk: OFF" and "Section box: OFF" Overlay Labels**

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`
- **Walk button**: Line 1057-1069
  - Label text "Walk: ON/OFF": Line 1068
- **Section box button**: Line 1070-1082
  - Label text "Section box: ON/OFF": Line 1081
- **Section box dimensions chip**: Line 1083-1090

**Supporting file for Section box logic**:
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/sectionBox.ts`
  - SectionBox class: Line 38-117
  - Summary method (produces readable dimensions): Line 82-87

### 3. **Canvas Overlay/HUD Container**

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`
- **Main canvas container**: Line 1035-1095
- **ViewCube overlay container**: Line 1048-1054 (top-right, z-20, absolute positioned)
- **Walk/Section box toolbar container**: Line 1056-1091 (bottom-left, z-20, absolute positioned, flex column)
- **Canvas mount point**: Line 1093 (Three.js render target)

### 4. **OrbitViewpointPersistedHud Component** (saved viewpoint metadata overlay)

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx`
- **Main component**: Line 44-233
- **HUD container**: Line 134-136 (bottom-right, z-10, absolute positioned)
- **Read-only display mode**: Line 145-165
- **Editable mode**: Line 166-224

### 5. **ToolPalette Component** (floating tool buttons toolbar)

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/tools/ToolPalette.tsx`
- **Main component**: Line 35-118
- **Toolbar container**: Line 67-118 (role="toolbar", pill shape, z-index: 10 via CSS)
- **Individual tool buttons**: Line 80-115

**Used in**:
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`
  - Positioned overlay wrapper: Line 878-893 (top-center, absolute, z-index: 10)

### Layout Architecture Summary

The canvas overlay uses a nested absolute positioning model:

1. **Main container** (Viewport.tsx, line 1035-1038): `relative h-full w-full overflow-hidden bg-background`
2. **Three overlays at z-20** (highest priority):

5. **Canvas mount** (line 1093): The actual Three.js renderer target

All overlay components use `pointer-events-auto` or `pointer-events-none` to manage interaction layering.
